title: alyssum alpestre subsp. tortuosum
intro: Alyssum alpestre subsp. tortuosum, also known as mountain alyssum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its small, white, fragrant flowers and compact, spreading growth habit. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and treating skin conditions such as acne and eczema. The health benefits of alyssum alpestre subsp. tortuosum include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. Alyssum alpestre subsp. tortuosum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or dried herb for medicinal use, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and potential interactions with other medications.
